body:
## Summary

This PR fixes two runtime issues across the CardGame app:

1. Backend npm scripts now point to the actual server entry file (`src/index.js`) instead of `src/index.ts`.
2. Frontend module/config imports are aligned with ESM requirements, and round progression now advances only after a full non-lethal round resolves.

## What changed

- Updated `CardGame/backend/package.json` so the `start` and `dev` scripts run `src/index.js`.
- Marked the frontend package as ESM and updated PostCSS config/export syntax to match.
- Added explicit file extensions to frontend local imports where required by ESM resolution.
- Extracted round-advance logic into a small `shouldAdvanceRound` helper.
- Moved round incrementing so it happens after the boss response resolves, and only when neither the boss nor the player is defeated.
- Added frontend tests covering the round progression helper behavior.

## Why

The backend scripts were targeting a TypeScript entrypoint that does not match the current runtime file layout. On the frontend side, ESM-related mismatches could break local module/config loading, and the game was incrementing rounds too early during combat flow.

## Impact

- Backend start/dev commands should now resolve the correct entrypoint.
- Frontend tooling should behave consistently under ESM.
- Round count should better reflect completed turns and avoid advancing on lethal outcomes.

## Validation

- Ran `npm run lint` in `CardGame/backend` and found pre-existing unrelated ESLint errors in `src/lib/skills.ts`, `src/types/state.ts`, `tests/unit/deck.test.ts`, and `tests/unit/hand.test.ts`.
- Ran `npm test` in `CardGame/frontend` and confirmed 3/3 tests pass.
- Ran `npm run build` in `CardGame/frontend` and confirmed the Vite production build succeeds.
title: fix: correct backend entrypoint and frontend round flow
